goal: Implement supervised dead-man live preview (countdown, confirm, auto-revert) and enable it only for the subset of dead-man rows with a proven reversible runtime strategy.
deadManPreviewCandidatesNote: the two animation toggles plus cursor.inactive_timeout, promoted by its passed per-row live proof (see runtime-preview-input-cursor-live-proof-status report)

classificationRationale.candidates: the two animation toggles are visual, medium-risk, use the live-proven scalar runtime mechanism, and auto-revert reliably; the countdown covers their performance risk
classificationRationale.needsLiveProof: 63 input/cursor rows with proven scalar grammar: the runtime mechanism should apply, but changing input behavior mid-session can impair the devices used to confirm or revert, so live use requires per-row live proof; the supervised panel renders disarmed with the reason
classificationRationale.modelOnly: 5 source-backed string rows: quoted runtime strings are unproven for input devices
classificationRationale.blockedNoSafeMechanism: 8 rows with vector/list/monitor-name/path grammars that have no proven runtime-set representation
classificationRationale.monitorDisplay: monitor/display/render rows are not dead-man gated at all; they remain BlockedHighRisk in the capability matrix with no supervised panel (test-enforced)

keptPreviewsSurviveSessionDrop: explicitly Kept previews are left in place on session drop; only unconfirmed previews auto-revert
executionPath: UI action -> dead-man UI panel -> RuntimePreviewDeadManController -> supervised session/apply/revert in runtime_preview_executor -> runner

livePproof.result: run once: armed a supervised preview on animations.enabled against the live compositor, verified the change, let the countdown expire unconfirmed, and verified the timeout auto-revert restored the original value

sourceGuards: the dead-man module contains no command construction, no config-file writes, no reload path, and no active config references
UI code contains no hl.config strings, no Command::new, and no direct executor session calls; everything routes through the controller
only DeadManPreviewCandidate rows can arm; needs-live-proof, model-only, and blocked rows fail closed in the controller and render disarmed or blocked in the UI
monitor/display rows have no dead-man UI state at all
normal preview projections are unchanged: 341 rows, 135 enabled
gtkEvidence: the Animations detail pane shows the armed supervised panel: 'Dead-man preview required' badge, 'Preview with recovery' button, 'Keep changes', 'Revert now', Cancel, 'reverts automatically' warning, recovery instruction, and status line (DeadManPreviewDetail run, AT-SPI text)
a blocked display/render row shows 'Preview blocked: high-risk setting' with no supervised panel and no recovery button
the supported-row Save/Revert/Cancel controls and the Config readiness card remain present
aggregate safety flags realConfigEdited, runtimeMutated, hyprlandReloaded, and mutatingHyprctlUsed are all false
nextRecommendedWork: The per-row proof architecture is implemented (see runtime-preview-input-cursor-live-proof-status report). Continue promoting rows one passed proof at a time.
